summary: Improved matching in `src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c`, focused on capsule/capsule or capsule-collision helpers. Four near-matching functions reached 100% (`lbColl_8000A244`, `lbColl_8000A78C`, `lbColl_8000A95C`, `lbColl_8000AB2C`) and several larger functions improved but remained below 100%, especially `lbColl_80006094`, `lbColl_800067F8`, and `lbColl_80006E58`. The work mainly reshaped local variable lifetimes, stack layout, constant usage, and parameter naming rather than changing documented behavior.

key_files.role: Only changed file; capsule collision matching, parameter naming, Vec3/local reshaping, constants/assert cleanup.

what_changed: Renamed the static `lbColl_80006E58` parameters from generic `arg0`-style names to role-based names: `hit_start`, `hit_end`, `hurt_start`, `hurt_end`, `hit_closest`, `hurt_closest`, `hurt_mtx`, `out_contact_pos`, `out_overlap`, `hit_radius`, `hurt_radius`, and `broadphase_scale`.
Removed the unused inline `lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr` helper and its associated assert strings `lbColl_804D3700` and `lbColl_804D3708`, because the helper was no longer referenced.
Added a small `between(float x, float lo, float hi)` helper and changed `nearzero` to use direct literals `1e-5F` and `-1e-5F` in the excerpted code.

Reworked `lbColl_80005C44` by narrowing temporary lifetimes inside nested scopes, using scoped `d1_dot_d1`, `d1_dot_d2`, and `scale`, and replacing some volatile constant loads with direct `0.0f` and `1.0` comparisons.
Collapsed several explicit scalar x/y/z stack temporaries into `Vec3` locals in multiple functions, matching the PR body note about `lbColl_80005C44` and parallel-axes branches of `lbColl_80006E58`.
Adjusted stack padding in larger functions, including `lbColl_80006094` changing from `PAD_STACK(56)` to `PAD_STACK(80)` and `lbColl_800067F8` using `PAD_STACK(72)` in the diff excerpt.

Changed many clamp comparisons in collision parameter solves to use existing floating constants such as `lbColl_804D7A00`, `lbColl_804D7A08`, `lbColl_804D7A10`, and `lbColl_804D79F8` instead of direct casts like `(float) 1.0` and `(float) 0.0` in some hot matching sections.
Restructured closest-point/distance calculations with nested blocks, copied `Vec3` temporaries, and explicit `dot` variables to influence register allocation and stack layout.
Used `SQ(...)` in at least one squared-distance comparison in `lbColl_80005C44`.

PR body reports new 100% matches for `lbColl_8000A244`, `lbColl_8000A78C`, `lbColl_8000A95C`, and `lbColl_8000AB2C`; improvements but not full matches for `lbColl_80006094`, `lbColl_800067F8`, `lbColl_80006E58`, and `lbColl_8000A584`.
smart_moves: Gave `lbColl_80006E58` semantic parameter names that document the collision roles: hit capsule segment endpoints, hurt capsule segment endpoints, closest-point outputs, hurt matrix, contact output, overlap output, radii, and broadphase scale.
Used `Vec3` locals to replace loose scalar coordinate triplets where that better matched stack/register behavior and made geometric intent clearer.
Narrowed variable lifetimes with nested scopes so the compiler could allocate temporaries more like the target assembly.
Preserved or reintroduced specific sdata/sdata2 float constants in matching-sensitive branches where direct literals produced worse codegen.
Cached or reused `1.0` according to the PR body to avoid repeated reloads across `*_param = 1.0` branches in `lbColl_80006E58`.
Removed dead inline JObj matrix helper/assert strings after they became unreferenced, reducing misleading symbols in `lbcollision.c`.
Accepted that remaining sub-100% functions were blocked by residual float-register-allocation differences rather than overclaiming full behavioral understanding.
decomp_lessons: For collision math, naming endpoints and output vectors by geometric role can make large matching functions easier to reason about without requiring a full algorithm rewrite.
When a function is mostly matched but blocked by float register allocation, try scoped temporaries, nested blocks, and `Vec3` aggregate copies to tune local lifetimes.
Do not assume replacing symbolic float constants with literals always helps; in matching-sensitive code, using the same global/sdata constant can be necessary for codegen.
Conversely, some small helpers like `nearzero` may match better or read better with direct float literals when evidence supports it.
Stack padding changes are part of legitimate matching work in this codebase; record them alongside the local-variable reshaping that made them necessary.
Large geometric functions may improve incrementally: reaching 100% in small surrounding functions while leaving big closest-point solvers below 100% is still useful progress.
Removing unreferenced inline helpers and assert strings is acceptable when the diff evidence shows they are no longer referenced, but avoid inferring broader API removal beyond the file.
naming_conventions: Use `hit_*` for the attacking or hit capsule inputs and outputs, e.g. `hit_start`, `hit_end`, `hit_closest`, `hit_radius`.
Use `hurt_*` for the defending or hurt capsule inputs and related matrix/radius values, e.g. `hurt_start`, `hurt_end`, `hurt_closest`, `hurt_mtx`, `hurt_radius`.
Use `out_*` for output parameters, shown by `out_contact_pos` and `out_overlap`.
Use `*_scl` or `*_param` style names for clamped segment parameters/scales when solving closest points along segments.
Temporary vector copies may be named after their source/role (`arg4_offset`, `arg5_offset`, `arg2_copy`) when no stronger semantic name is proven.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced nested scopes to shorten live ranges for floats and vector copies.
Changed separate coordinate stack slots into `Vec3` aggregate locals to alter stack layout and loads/stores.
Adjusted `PAD_STACK` amounts in large functions to match expected stack frame shape.
Used existing float constants such as `lbColl_804D7A00`, `lbColl_804D7A08`, `lbColl_804D7A10`, `lbColl_804D79F8`, and `lbColl_804D7A18` in some branches to influence load behavior.
Folded assignment expressions in the closest-points solve according to the PR body: `hit_param = (hit_param_candidate = ...)`.
Cached `1.0` in a callee-save float in `lbColl_80006E58` according to the PR body, avoiding reloads across branches assigning `*_param = 1.0`.
Used explicit `dot` temporaries before division in closest-point calculations, likely to guide float-register scheduling.
Replaced repeated multiplication patterns with `SQ(...)` in at least one distance comparison.

review_feedback.text: Author noted the PR was opened prematurely.
Author said they would fix regressions.
Author reported editorconfig checks failed due to a network error downloading nix packages and could not be rerun locally.
No review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching `src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c` for final 100% matches of `lbColl_80006094`, `lbColl_800067F8`, and `lbColl_80006E58`.
Search for subsequent renames of `hit_*` and `hurt_*` capsule fields or parameters to confirm whether this naming convention persisted.
Look for PRs resolving residual float-register-allocation mismatches in closest-point segment/capsule collision code.
Verify whether `lbColl_JObjGetMtxPtr` stayed removed or was replaced by a shared JObj matrix access helper elsewhere.
